searchPlaceholder: Search by IP, DNS, CIDR, HTTP, TLS, SSH, or port
emptyDescription: Try a broader term such as IP, DNS, CIDR, port, HTTP, TLS, or SSH.

ip.label: IP and CIDR
ip.description: Inspect public addresses, normalize ranges, and convert between CIDR, IPv4, IPv6, and MAC-derived formats.

dns.label: DNS and Domains
dns.description: Query DNS records, reverse-resolve IP addresses, and convert internationalized domain names.

reference.label: Protocol References
reference.description: Look up HTTP status codes, MIME types, port numbers, and network clock information.

security.label: Keys and Certificates
security.description: Inspect TLS certificate details and generate fingerprints for SSH public keys.

certificate-public-key-parser.name: X.509 Certificate & Public Key Parser
certificate-public-key-parser.description: Parse X.509 certificates and public keys in PEM, DER, or base64 DER form. Review subjects, issuers, validity dates, fingerprints, extensions, and key details locally in your browser.

cidr-parser.name: CIDR Parser
cidr-parser.description: Parse CIDR notation to inspect IPv4 or IPv6 network ranges, masks, and integer boundaries directly in the browser.

cidrs-merger-excluder.name: CIDRs Merger & Excluder
cidrs-merger-excluder.description: Merge IPv4 and IPv6 CIDR blocks, subtract excluded networks, and copy the minimal resulting CIDR list.

current-network-time.name: Current Network Time
current-network-time.description: Get the current network time and compare it with your local system time. View accurate network time and detect any time differences.

dns-lookup.name: DNS Lookup
dns-lookup.description: Query A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, NS, SOA, CAA, SRV, HTTPS, and SVCB DNS records through DNS over HTTPS resolvers.

http-status-code-lookup.name: HTTP Status Code Lookup
http-status-code-lookup.description: Search and browse HTTP status codes and their meanings.

ip-cidr-normalizer.name: IP/CIDR Normalizer
ip-cidr-normalizer.description: Normalize IP addresses and CIDR notation for both IPv4 and IPv6 formats. Enter any IP or CIDR range to get the standardized format instantly.

ip-info-lookup.name: IP Info Lookup
ip-info-lookup.description: Look up geolocation, ISP, ASN, reverse DNS, and network details for an IP address, domain, or URL.

ip-range-to-cidr-converter.name: IP Range to CIDR Converter
ip-range-to-cidr-converter.description: Convert IPv4 or IPv6 start and end addresses into the minimal set of CIDR blocks that exactly covers the range.

ipv6-address-to-mac-address-converter.name: IPv6 Address to MAC Address Converter
ipv6-address-to-mac-address-converter.description: Convert IPv6 addresses to MAC addresses. Enter any IPv6 address to extract the corresponding MAC address instantly.

mac-address-to-ipv6-link-local-address-c.name: MAC Address to IPv6 Link-Local Address Converter
mac-address-to-ipv6-link-local-address-c.description: Convert MAC addresses to IPv6 link-local addresses. Enter any MAC address and optionally add an interface name to generate the scoped result instantly.

mime-type-lookup.name: MIME Type Lookup
mime-type-lookup.description: Search and browse MIME types and their file extensions.

my-ip-address.name: My IP Address
my-ip-address.description: Discover your public IP address and get detailed information about your internet connection including geolocation, ISP details, and network information.

port-number-lookup.name: Port Number Lookup
port-number-lookup.description: Search and browse common network port numbers.

reverse-ip-lookup.name: Reverse IP Lookup
reverse-ip-lookup.description: Look up reverse DNS PTR records for IPv4 or IPv6 addresses through public DNS-over-HTTPS resolvers directly in the browser.

ssh-public-key-fingerprint.name: SSH Public Key Fingerprint
ssh-public-key-fingerprint.description: Generate SHA-256 and MD5 fingerprints for OpenSSH public keys, authorized_keys lines, and SSH2 public key blocks.

unicode-punycode-converter.name: Unicode ↔ Punycode Converter
unicode-punycode-converter.description: Convert Unicode (IDN) and ASCII (Punycode) domains both ways.
